mission.title: kubeflow: Running kfctl apply k8s fails on 2nd application
mission.description: Running kfctl apply k8s fails on 2nd application. This issue affects 5+ users.

steps.title: Identify kubeflow troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your kubeflow deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review kubeflow configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant kubeflow configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow
kubectl get configmap -n kubeflow -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=kubeflow
```
**What steps did you take and what happened:**
I ran `kfctl apply k8s` a 2nd time and it failed saying istio had already bound the port.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Running kfctl apply k8s fails on 2nd application
steps.description:
+1 to that. I think coloring the did to apply is getting hairy and will end
up replicating kubectl.

On Fri, Aug 9, 2019 at 1:55 PM Matt Brown <notifications.com> wrote:

> Has it ever been considered to shell out to kubectl apply ... rather than
> have client-go code in kfctl that talks to the k8s
```yaml
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
namespace: kf-demo
resources:
- https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/blob/master/pipeline/pipelines-ui/base/kustomization.yaml
generators:
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joe/pipelines/app.yaml
```

steps.title: Confirm Running kfctl apply k8s fails on 2nd application is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=kubeflow -n kubeflow --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n kubeflow --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
+1 to that. I think coloring the did to apply is getting hairy and will end
up replicating kubectl.

On Fri, Aug 9, 2019 at 1:55 PM Matt Brown <notifications.com> wrote:

> Has it ever been considered to shell out to kubectl apply ... rather than
> have client-go code in kfctl that talks to the k8s api-server directly?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: kustomize.config.k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Kustomization
namespace: kf-demo
resources:
- https://github.com/kubeflow/manifests/blob/master/pipeline/pipelines-ui/base/kustomization.yaml
generators:
- https://raw.githubusercontent.com/joe/pipelines/app.yaml

apiVersion: kfdef.apps.kubeflow.org/v1alpha1
kind: KfDef
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
  name: dls-kf
  namespace: kubeflow
spec:
  appdir: /Users/joe/kf
  applications:
  - kustomizeConfig:
      overlays:
      - istio
      repoRef:
        name: manifests
        path: pipeline/pipelines-ui
    name: pipelines-ui

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with kubeflow installed or the issue environment reproducible.
